description: Integrate Dagster as the ETL orchestration layer — wrap existing scraper and transform functions as software-defined assets with full lineage, selective materialisation per content type, and a weekly schedule.

userStories.title: Scaffold pipeline/ module and empty Definitions entry point
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a Dagster code location wired into the project so that dg dev discovers all assets.
acceptanceCriteria: New pipeline/ Python package exists at project root with __init__.py
pipeline/defs/ subdirectory exists with sub-modules: assets/__init__.py, resources.py, jobs.py, schedules.py
Root-level definitions.py exports a symbol named defs of type dagster.Definitions (empty — no assets registered yet)
If dg dev does not auto-discover definitions.py, pyproject.toml gets a [tool.dagster] section with python_file = 'definitions.py'
dg dev starts without import errors and the Dagster UI loads at http://localhost:3000
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Refactor transform_opportunity_website() for per-type invocation
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the monolithic transform_opportunity_website() to accept a content_type parameter so each content type can be normalised independently without processing all types.
acceptanceCriteria: transforms/sources/opportunity_website.py: transform_opportunity_website() accepts an optional content_type: str | None = None parameter
When content_type is provided, only that type's items are processed and written to data/clean/{type}/ — other directories are not touched
Supported content_type values match the existing type slugs: 'policy', 'team', 'blog', 'events', 'party-information', 'pdf-document'
When content_type is None (default), all types are processed — existing behaviour is fully preserved
Existing call in transforms/main.py (no content_type argument) continues to work and produces identical output
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Create ingestion assets — raw scraper layer
userStories.description: As a pipeline operator, I want each scraper to be a Dagster asset so I can materialise raw data independently per content type.
acceptanceCriteria: pipeline/defs/assets/ingestion.py defines six @dg.asset functions assigned to group 'ingestion': raw_policies, raw_team, raw_blog, raw_events, raw_party_info, raw_pdfs
Each asset wraps the corresponding scrape_* and save_* pair from scraper/ without duplicating their logic
raw_pdfs declares raw_policies as an upstream non-argument dependency via deps=['raw_policies'] or non_argument_deps={'raw_policies'}
Each asset returns a dagster.MaterializeResult with metadata: item_count (int) and output_path (str)
All six assets are registered in the defs Definitions object in definitions.py
Running dg dev and opening the UI shows all six assets under group ingestion
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Create clean layer assets — normalise to data/clean/
userStories.description: As a pipeline operator, I want each content type's normalisation step to be a Dagster asset so I can re-clean a single type without re-scraping.
acceptanceCriteria: pipeline/defs/assets/clean.py defines six @dg.asset functions in group 'clean': clean_policies, clean_team, clean_blog, clean_events, clean_party_info, clean_pdfs
Each clean asset declares its corresponding raw_* asset as an upstream dependency
Each clean asset calls transform_opportunity_website(content_type=<type>) to process only that content type
Materialising clean_blog writes only data/clean/blog/ and does not touch any other clean directory
Each asset returns a dagster.MaterializeResult with metadata: item_count (int) and output_path (str)
All six assets are registered in the defs Definitions object in definitions.py
All six assets appear in the Dagster UI under group clean with correct upstream edges to their ingestion assets
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Create site layer assets — generate site/src/content/
userStories.description: As a pipeline operator, I want each site-content collection to be a Dagster asset so the Astro site can be rebuilt per content type without clobbering other collections.
acceptanceCriteria: pipeline/defs/assets/site.py defines five @dg.asset functions in group 'site': site_policies, site_team, site_blog, site_events, site_party_info
Each site asset declares its corresponding clean_* asset as an upstream dependency
Each site asset wipes only its own collection subdirectory (e.g. site/src/content/blog/) before writing — not the entire site/src/content/ tree
Each site asset calls the corresponding transform_<type>(CLEAN_DIR, CONTENT_DIR) function from transforms/
Materialising site_blog writes only site/src/content/blog/ and does not delete or overwrite other collections
Each asset returns a dagster.MaterializeResult with metadata: item_count (int) and output_path (str)
All five assets are registered in the defs Definitions object in definitions.py
The full three-layer graph (ingestion → clean → site) is visible in the Dagster UI lineage view
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Create clean_index asset — search index regeneration
userStories.description: As a downstream consumer, I need data/clean/_index.json to stay current whenever clean assets are materialised.
acceptanceCriteria: A clean_index @dg.asset is defined in group 'clean' (in pipeline/defs/assets/clean.py or a dedicated file)
clean_index declares all six clean_* assets as upstream dependencies
clean_index calls the existing index-writing logic (e.g. _write_index() or equivalent from transforms/sources/opportunity_website.py) to regenerate data/clean/_index.json
clean_index is registered in the defs Definitions object in definitions.py
clean_index appears downstream of all six clean assets in the Dagster UI asset graph
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Create named jobs and weekly schedule
userStories.description: As an operator, I want named jobs for common pipeline patterns and a weekly schedule so content refresh can be automated.
acceptanceCriteria: pipeline/defs/jobs.py defines three jobs using dg.define_asset_job: full_pipeline (selection='*'), ingestion_job (selection=dg.AssetSelection.groups('ingestion')), transforms_job (selection=dg.AssetSelection.groups('clean', 'site'))
pipeline/defs/schedules.py defines weekly_full_pipeline using dg.ScheduleDefinition targeting full_pipeline with cron_schedule='0 6 * * 1'
Schedule has no default_status override — it is OFF by default
All three jobs and the schedule are registered in the defs Definitions object in definitions.py
All three jobs appear in the Dagster UI under Jobs
weekly_full_pipeline appears in the Dagster UI under Automation → Schedules in the off/paused state
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Update justfile to use Dagster CLI commands
userStories.description: As a developer, I want the just task runner to invoke Dagster so all pipeline execution goes through one system.
acceptanceCriteria: justfile scrape recipe body is replaced with: uv run dagster asset materialize --select 'group:ingestion'
justfile transform recipe body is replaced with: uv run dagster asset materialize --select 'group:clean group:site'
New justfile dev recipe added with body: uv run dg dev
Old uv run python main.py and uv run python -m transforms.main invocations are removed from the scrape and transform recipes
Running just --list shows scrape, transform, and dev recipes
Typecheck passes
